decisionQuestion: Which CycleWarden workstream should be prioritized next among #15 standalone core, minimal #13 candidate-grounded research, remaining #12 sandbox hardening, and #17 governed execution?
protectedConstraints: Recruit exactly 6 external developers across exactly 6 repositories.
Run the external pilot for no more than 14 calendar days.
Do not count internal calibration as external decision value.
Do not expand #12 or #17 unless the pilot threat model or participant workflow demonstrates a blocker.

protectedExperiment.name: A2 Research Audit external decision-value pilot
successCriteria: At least 3 of 6 audits create decision value.
At least 4 of 6 participants can explain and challenge the ranking.
No repeated serious evidence, privacy, or safety failure occurs.
failureCriteria: At most 1 of 6 audits creates decision value.
A serious evidence, privacy, or safety failure repeats across participants.

candidates.title: Standalone Evolution Core
candidates.problem: External pilots require a core that installs, tests, packs, and runs without the web application dependency tree.
candidates.expectedOutcome: A reproducible package boundary for external repository use.

evidence.title: Issue #15 completion snapshot
evidence.statement: Issue #15 is closed completed after standalone Node 20 and Node 22 package proofs.

evidence.title: Standalone core roadmap checkpoint
evidence.statement: The pre-audit sequence placed standalone core before candidate grounding and the external pilot.

evidence.title: Evolution state machine capability
evidence.statement: The standalone package exposes the deterministic lifecycle core.

evidence.title: Merged standalone core implementation
evidence.statement: PR #27 merged the independent package toolchain, tarball consumer proof, and npm CLI entrypoints.

candidates.smallestExperiment: No new experiment; preserve the completed milestone as a rejected alternative.
candidates.hypothesis: Completed work should remain visible but must not be selected again.
acceptanceCriteria: The candidate remains rejected because its issue is completed.
rollbackPlan: Reopen #15 only if the verified package boundary regresses.

candidates.title: Minimal candidate-grounded research
candidates.problem: The current repository adapter substitutes a generic opportunity portfolio when the decision names concrete alternatives.
candidates.expectedOutcome: Every named alternative has inspectable issue, roadmap, capability, and implementation evidence or the run returns inconclusive.

evidence.title: Issue #29 candidate-grounding requirement
evidence.statement: Issue #29 requires named candidate evidence, stale capability rejection, protected pilot parameters, and an explainable ranking or inconclusive outcome.

evidence.title: Calibration follow-up sequence
evidence.statement: The calibration retained minimal #13 evidence work before the fixed external pilot and deferred broader research expansion.

evidence.title: Research intelligence capability
evidence.statement: Research intelligence is partial and lacks decision-candidate grounding and real-user proof.

evidence.title: Current generic repository research adapter
evidence.statement: The existing adapter always constructs three generic opportunities and cannot bind them to named alternatives.

alternatives: Proceed to external pilot with generic rankings
Build broad web search before candidate grounding
candidates.smallestExperiment: Rerun the fixed four-candidate decision through the manifest adapter and require candidate-specific evidence or a durable inconclusive result.
candidates.hypothesis: A typed candidate evidence map will explain why #13 outranks remaining #12 and #17 work without changing the fixed #14 protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: The fixed run preserves all four named alternatives.
Each alternative links all four required evidence categories.
The external pilot parameters remain byte-for-byte represented in the manifest evidence.
verificationPlan: Persist and reload one candidate CLI cycle.
Run the fixed CycleWarden candidate calibration fixture.
rollbackPlan: Remove the candidate adapter and retain issue #29 plus the original calibration evidence.

candidates.title: Remaining sandbox hardening
candidates.problem: Hard workspace quota, restricted egress, remote providers, cross-platform support, and external review remain open.
candidates.expectedOutcome: Broader sandbox guarantees for higher exposure environments.

evidence.title: Issue #12 remaining sandbox work
evidence.statement: Issue #12 remains open for guarantees beyond the verified local Docker baseline.

evidence.title: Exposure-based sandbox sequencing
evidence.statement: Only exposure-blocking sandbox work should precede the local pilot; broader hardening is deferred.

evidence.title: Sandboxed agent execution capability
evidence.statement: A verified Docker baseline exists while broader guarantees remain limited.

evidence.title: Merged Docker sandbox baseline
evidence.statement: The current implementation already provides explicit trusted-local and hostile-tested Docker backends.

alternatives: Treat the current local baseline as sufficient for the bounded pilot
Delay all value validation until remote sandbox support exists
candidates.smallestExperiment: Ask each pilot repository whether the local trust model is a participation blocker before adding broader sandbox features.
candidates.hypothesis: The remaining sandbox work is not a blocker for the bounded local pilot unless participant threat models show otherwise.
acceptanceCriteria: A participant or threat-model finding identifies an exposure blocker.
verificationPlan: Record pilot repository trust requirements separately from feature preferences.
rollbackPlan: Keep network denied and do not enable unverified exposure modes.

candidates.title: Governed coding-agent execution
candidates.problem: No coding-agent adapter consumes an approved ExecutionHandoff and independent verifier verdict yet.
candidates.expectedOutcome: A thin real-agent path from approved handoff to sandboxed change and draft PR.

evidence.title: Issue #17 governed agent adapters
evidence.statement: Issue #17 requires approved handoff consumption, isolated execution, manifests, cancellation, and draft-only delivery.

evidence.title: Governed execution sequencing gate
evidence.statement: Governed execution begins only after the external decision-value pilot passes.

evidence.title: Sandbox foundation without agent consumption
evidence.statement: The sandbox foundation is partial because no coding-agent adapter consumes ExecutionHandoff.

evidence.title: Execution backend boundary
evidence.statement: Execution backends run commands but no agent adapter currently consumes an approved research handoff.

alternatives: Validate decision value before implementing agent delivery
Build multiple agent adapters before testing one real path
candidates.smallestExperiment: After #14 passes, run one approved handoff through one thin real-agent adapter and an independent verifier.
candidates.hypothesis: A real-agent integration should shape the adapter contract only after CycleWarden proves the decision is worth implementing.
acceptanceCriteria: The external pilot has passed its precommitted success rule.
One real agent produces a bounded draft-only change through the selected backend.
verificationPlan: Verify handoff digest and exact scope before execution.
Require an independent verdict before draft PR creation.
rollbackPlan: Cancel the execution, delete the isolated worktree, and retain the failed attempt as evidence.
